claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let me help you check your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, I need to know about your child (or children):

**How old is your child?**

- Are they under 16?
- Are they 16-19 years old?
- Are they 20 or over?

Please tell me your child's age, and if you have multiple children, let me know about each of them and we'll check eligibility for all of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I have one child - Alex is 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me check the eligibility for Alex, who is 13 years old.

Since Alex is under 16, they potentially qualify as a qualifying young person. Now I need to check a few more things.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

In other words, does Alex either:
- Not have a paid job, OR
- Work less than 24 hours per week?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't have a job at all. They're only 13, so they're just in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I'd expect for a 13-year-old. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

This means Alex is not claiming benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit in their own name, correct?

For a 13-year-old in school, this would typically be the case - they wouldn't be receiving these types of benefits.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

Since Alex is 13 and in regular school, I assume they're not in an apprenticeship program. Can you confirm that Alex is just in regular school and not doing any apprenticeship?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex is just in regular school, no apprenticeship or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Now let me move on to check your eligibility as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check if you meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

This is the most common way to meet the residency requirement. If you don't normally live in the UK, there are other special circumstances that might qualify you (such as having settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or being a Crown servant abroad).

Do you normally live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since you don't normally live in the UK, let me check if you meet any of the special circumstances that would still qualify you for Child Benefit.

**Do any of these situations apply to you?**

1. **Crown servant abroad**: Are you living abroad as a Crown servant (e.g., working for the UK government, diplomatic service, armed forces)?

2. **EU Settlement Scheme - Settled Status**: Do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?

3. **EU Settlement Scheme - Pre-settled Status**: Do you have pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme? (If yes, there are additional criteria you'd need to meet, such as working, studying, or actively seeking work)

Which, if any, of these applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply to me. I'm just living abroad - not a Crown servant, and I don't have settled or pre-settled status.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Based on the information you've provided, I need to give you the outcome of this assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment Result

I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for Alex at this time.

### Why you're not eligible:

While **Alex qualifies** as a qualifying young person (they're 13 years old, in regular school, not working, and not receiving benefits in their own right), **you do not meet the UK residency requirements** for Child Benefit.

### Residency Requirements:

To claim Child Benefit, you must either:
- Normally live in the UK, OR
- Meet special circumstances such as:
  - Having settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
  - Having pre-settled status with qualifying criteria (working, studying, or having sufficient resources)
  - Being a Crown servant living abroad
  - Living in certain qualifying countries

Since you're living abroad and don't meet any of these special circumstances, you cannot claim Child Benefit at this time.

### What you could consider:

- If you move back to the UK and normally live there, you would then meet the residency requirements and could claim Child Benefit for Alex
- If your circumstances change (for example, if you become a Crown servant or gain settled/pre-settled status), you may become eligible

For more information, you can visit: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility

I hope this helps clarify your situation. If your circumstances change in the future, please feel free to check your eligibility again.

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old and meets all the child-related criteria: they are under 16, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in a disqualifying apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and does not meet any of the special circumstances that would qualify them (they are not a Crown servant, do not have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and do not have pre-settled status). UK Child Benefit requires the claimant to either normally live in the UK or meet specific special circumstances, which is not satisfied in this case.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (13 years old), who qualifies as a qualifying young person. However, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. They live abroad and do not qualify under any of the special circumstances (Crown servant, settled status, or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme).

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and does not meet any of the special circumstances that would qualify them (they are not a Crown servant, do not have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and do not have pre-settled status). UK Child Benefit requires the claimant to either normally live in the UK or meet specific special circumstances, which is not satisfied in this case.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (13 years old), who qualifies as a qualifying young person. However, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. They live abroad and do not qualify under any of the special circumstances (Crown servant, settled status, or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme).
